title: 2.6 Seed Dispersal and Germination
description: Seed dispersal and germination — every fact from the textbook.

questions.question: At what stage do plants display long-distance mobility?
What is seed dispersal?
options: An adaptive mechanism ensuring seeds are separated from parent plants and distributed over a large area
questions.correctAnswer: An adaptive mechanism ensuring seeds are separated from parent plants and distributed over a large area

questions.question: What does seed dispersal help safeguard?
options: Germination and survival of seeds to adult plants, minimizing overcrowding
questions.correctAnswer: Germination and survival of seeds to adult plants, minimizing overcrowding

questions.question: How can seeds be dispersed?
How do seeds in fleshy fruits get dispersed by birds?
options: Birds eat the fruit and the seeds fall directly
The seeds are ingested, escape digestion due to hard seed coats, and are dropped at a distance upon defecation
questions.correctAnswer: The seeds are ingested, escape digestion due to hard seed coats, and are dropped at a distance upon defecation

questions.question: What adaptation allows seeds to be dispersed by wind or water?
How are seeds that stick or cling to fur or clothes dispersed?
What type of seeds act as kites or propellers?
options: Seeds within fruits that act as kites or propellers
Seeds that are ingested by birds
questions.correctAnswer: Seeds within fruits that act as kites or propellers

questions.question: What is the first outward sign of growth during seed germination?
options: A radicle that develops from the hypocotyl
questions.correctAnswer: A radicle that develops from the hypocotyl

questions.question: What does the radicle grow down to become?
What is the root essential for?
options: Anchorage and access to water and nutrients from the soil
questions.correctAnswer: Anchorage and access to water and nutrients from the soil

questions.question: What does the shoot that develops from the plumule do?
What is the resumption of active growth of the embryo after dormancy called?
What are the three essential conditions for seed germination?
Why is water important for seed germination?
options: Hydration of seed coat increases permeability to oxygen, enables enzymatic hydrolysis and acts as transport agent
questions.correctAnswer: Hydration of seed coat increases permeability to oxygen, enables enzymatic hydrolysis and acts as transport agent

questions.question: Why is oxygen necessary for seed germination?
options: For aerobic respiration by which seeds get energy for embryo growth
questions.correctAnswer: For aerobic respiration by which seeds get energy for embryo growth

questions.question: What temperature condition do seeds require for germination?
What feature differentiates hypogeal germination from epigeal germination?
options: The position and fate of the cotyledon
questions.correctAnswer: The position and fate of the cotyledon

questions.question: In epigeal germination, what happens to the cotyledon?
options: It is pushed up to become the photosynthetic surface of the seedling
questions.correctAnswer: It is pushed up to become the photosynthetic surface of the seedling

questions.question: In hypogeal germination, what happens to the cotyledon?
What does seed germination cover from start to finish?
options: From seed formation to fruit ripening
From the time seed embryo starts growth up to formation of young independent plant with photosynthetic leaves
questions.correctAnswer: From the time seed embryo starts growth up to formation of young independent plant with photosynthetic leaves

questions.question: What is the length of time seeds are typically allowed to germinate in the textbook experiment?
What does the oil layer in the germination experiment demonstrate?
What does the ice cube in the germination experiment demonstrate?
What does wet versus dry cotton wool in the germination experiment demonstrate?
